subject: Religious Education
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 1.4 CHOOSING AND TALENTS
topics: Choosing and talents are like the two sides of a coin as they cannot be separated. They both
play important roles in people’s lives. Life is full of choices, as a result people make choices
throughout their life time. Some of the choices people make are good and others are bad, hence
we have to follow all the necessary steps in order to make good choices. Choices can be made
activities: have been designed to promote practical demonstrations with a view that learners will develop
competences that will help them in life.
In this topic, there are three sub-topics. These are Choices, Choices in the four religions in
Zambia and Talents. From the presentations, teachers are expected to gain understanding and
skills in order to present the topic effectively and confidently.
Analyse central teachings on making choices in the four
resources: The main aim of a Hindu is to be free from all selfishness. This is because Hindus
believe that sin and suffering come about from people’s evil desires. The Bhagavad Gita
says: ‘The person who gives up all desires without thinking ‘mine’ or ‘I’ is the one who
assessment:  Learners to evaluate a given situation where steps for decision making are followed
in arriving at the final decision and action
 Groups to present their findings inform of reports to the teacher
 Teacher assesses the report and discusses them with the learners
 Teacher to emphasise the importance of each step in decision making in life
